title: Drought Early Warning And Food Insecurity Prediction In The Horn Of Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for assessing where rainfall, vegetation, and market-price signals provide usable lead time for food insecurity early warning in the Horn of Africa.
why_it_matters: The Horn of Africa experienced its worst drought in 40 years during 2020-2023, pushing over 23 million people into acute food insecurity across Ethiopia, Somalia, and Kenya. FEWS NET and IPC provide early warning, but connecting rainfall and vegetation signals to food-insecurity outcomes with verified lead times at sub-national scale remains underdeveloped.

success_metrics.target: Every food-insecurity predictor links to dated evidence with method and limitations.
Candidate signals report lead-time distributions with false-alarm rates.
No food-aid recommendation merged without review and replication.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status and review records.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
